task_completion.description: Agent opens the observation request file to read the target ephemeris and measurement protocol.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of /home/ga/Documents/neo_obs_request.txt.

task_completion.description: Agent slews the simulated telescope to the target coordinates for 2020 QG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Telescope control software (e.g., KStars, Ekos, or INDI control panel) showing the mount's current position at approximately RA 16h 52m (16.883h) and Dec -21d 55m (-21.917 deg).

task_completion.description: Agent configures the CCD simulator with the correct exposure time and save directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Camera/Imaging control interface showing the exposure time set to 30 seconds and the output/save directory set to /home/ga/Images/asteroids/2020QG.

task_completion.description: Agent captures the required minimum number of FITS images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An imaging sequence showing at least 6 completed frames, or a file manager/terminal showing 6 or more .fits files successfully saved inside /home/ga/Images/asteroids/2020QG.

task_completion.description: Agent produces the final MPC-format astrometry report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor displaying the file /home/ga/Documents/mpc_report.txt containing MPC-formatted astrometric data (typically fixed-width columns including the designation '2020 QG', observation dates/times, RA, and Dec).

integrity.description: Verifies the agent used actual astronomy software to control the telescope and camera.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of astronomy software GUIs (like KStars, Ekos, or INDI Web Manager) actively being used, rather than terminal scripts mocking the INDI server or bypassing the simulation.

integrity.description: Verifies FITS files were generated by the CCD simulator.
integrity.visual_evidence: The capture process is visible in the astronomy software's imaging module. The agent should not be using commands like 'touch' or downloading fake .fits files from the internet.

integrity.description: Verifies the MPC report contains actual observational data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mpc_report.txt contains specific astrometric data lines (e.g., timestamps, measured RA/Dec) rather than just repeating the task prompt or containing placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target: 2020 QG. Required coordinates: RA 16h 52m 58s (16.883h), Dec -21d 55m 00s (-21.917 deg) with a 2.0-degree tolerance. Camera settings: 30-second exposure. Image save directory: /home/ga/Images/asteroids/2020QG. Minimum images required: 6 FITS files. Final report path: /home/ga/Documents/mpc_report.txt.
